bill.short_title: Extension of Continuing Appropriations and Other Matters Act, 2024

provisions.description: Strikes the date specified in section 106(3) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 and inserts 'March 22, 2024', extending the overall CR duration.

metadata.llm_classification: cr_date_extension — amends the primary CR end date in section 106(3) of P.L. 118-15 to extend funding through March 22, 2024

provisions.raw_text: by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting ''March 22, 2024''
notes: Section 106(3) of P.L. 118-15 specifies the general expiration date of the continuing resolution. This paragraph extends it to March 22, 2024.
This is paragraph (1) of SEC. 101, which amends division A of Public Law 118-15.

cross_references.description: Extends the CR end date to March 22, 2024

provisions.description: Strikes the date specified in section 106(4) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 and inserts 'March 8, 2024'.

metadata.llm_classification: cr_date_extension — amends the secondary date in section 106(4) of P.L. 118-15 to March 8, 2024

provisions.raw_text: by striking the date specified in section 106(4) and inserting ''March 8, 2024''
notes: Section 106(4) of P.L. 118-15 specifies a secondary date within the CR, typically for a subset of appropriations or a different funding ladder step.
This is paragraph (2) of SEC. 101, which amends division A of Public Law 118-15.
The March 8 date is earlier than the March 22 general CR expiration, suggesting a two-step funding ladder structure.

cross_references.description: Changes the secondary CR date to March 8, 2024

provisions.description: Amends the FAFSA student available income formula in Section 475(g)(1) of the Higher Education Act to set the income assessment at 50% of the difference between total income and adjustments, with a floor of -$1,500 for award year 2024-2025 and zero for 2025-2026 and thereafter.
provisions.raw_text: Section 475(g)(1) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1087oo(g)(1)), as amended by the FAFSA Simplification Act (title VII of division FF o
notes: Subsection (a) of SEC. 101
Modifies the student available income calculation to allow a negative floor of -$1,500 for award year 2024-2025, effectively increasing Pell Grant eligibility

cross_references.target: Section 475(g)(1) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1087oo(g)(1))
cross_references.description: As previously amended by the FAFSA Simplification Act (title VII of division FF of P.L. 116-260)

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from Statutory PAYGO scorecards, Senate PAYGO scorecards, and budget scorekeeping rules including section 251 sequestration, section 302(a) allocations, and classification as an appropriation Act under the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act.
provisions.raw_text: The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
notes: Comprehensive PAYGO and budget scoring exemption for the Pell Grant mandatory spending provisions in Division B
Ensures the mandatory Pell Grant amounts do not trigger sequestration or count against appropriations committee allocations
Exempts from: Statutory PAYGO (subsection a), Senate PAYGO (subsection b), and budget classification rules (subsection c)

cross_references.target: Section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)
Rule 3 of Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines (Conference Report 105-217)
Section 250(c)(8) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985
Section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985
Section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974
Section 3(4)(C) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
cross_references.description: Not classified as appropriation Act spending

flagged_issues: Division A contains no new dollar amounts — it solely extends the dates of the existing CR (P.L. 118-15, Division A).
SEC. 101 creates a two-step CR expiration: March 8, 2024 (sec 106(4)) and March 22, 2024 (sec 106(3)).
Division B ('Other Matters') was not extracted per the instruction scope.
SEC. 101(b) appropriates a total of $7,746,000,000 in mandatory Pell Grant funding across FY2024-FY2027, with $1,236,000,000/year permanently thereafter
SEC. 102 exempts all Division B budgetary effects from PAYGO and budget scoring, effectively treating the mandatory Pell spending as budget-neutral for enforcement purposes
All mandatory_spending_extension amounts use semantics 'mandatory_spending' and are not counted in total_budget_authority per extraction rules
